task_completion.description: GIMP is open with the original landscape image loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible, displaying a landscape photograph on the canvas.
A dialog box titled 'Add Border' is visible on the screen, typically accessed via Filters -> Decor -> Add Border.

task_completion.description: The border size settings are configured within the acceptable range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Add Border' dialog, the Border X size and Border Y size are set to values between 5 and 100 pixels (which corresponds to a total dimension increase of 10 to 200 pixels).

task_completion.description: The decorative border is successfully applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main GIMP canvas shows the landscape image with a newly added, uniform decorative border around all its edges.

task_completion.description: The modified image is exported as 'bordered_image.jpg' to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename 'bordered_image.jpg' and the destination folder set to Desktop, or the file 'bordered_image.jpg' is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIMP graphical interface rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being used to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in Add Border filter rather than manually drawing a border.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Add Border' dialog is seen, and the resulting border is perfectly uniform, rather than being manually painted with brush or bucket fill tools.

integrity.description: The original image content is preserved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The central part of the image remains the original landscape photograph and has not been replaced, heavily distorted, or obscured by the border.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is an 800x533 landscape photograph. The agent must use GIMP's 'Add Border' filter (Filters -> Decor -> Add Border). The border size should increase the image dimensions by 10 to 200 pixels in total for each dimension (i.e., 5 to 100 pixels per side). The final image must be exported as 'bordered_image.jpg' to the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/).
